title: Tecoma radicans princei
intro: Tecoma radicans princei, also known as the Trumpet Vine or Prince's Trumpet Vine, is a climbing plant native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, which include the treatment of fever, cough, and respiratory issues, as well as its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Tecoma radicans princei include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma.
taxonomy: Tecoma radicans princei is a cultivar of the Tecoma radicans species, which is a type of flowering plant. Tecoma radicans princei is classified as a member of the Bignoniaceae family.
